notes: morning. so were on the pacific highway job today, doing the kerb and gutter on the southbound lane from chainage 450 to about 520
traffic control is set up, looks good. two lanes closed, one open with a 40 zone. the TCP was approved last week
weather is absolute rubbish today. been raining since like 4am. the trench is full of water
pumps are running trying to get the water out of the trench. got 2 pumps going, 3 inch and a 4 inch
the excavator operator Steve is on site. CAT 320, been using it to dig the kerb trench. but cant do much till the water is out
surveyor is here pegging out the next section of kerb from chainage 520 to 600
the string line is set up for the first 30 meters of kerb. form work going in now. Kevs crew is on it, 4 guys
concrete truck is booked for 10am but honestly with this rain I dont know if we'll be ready. might have to push to after lunch
called the batch plant, pushed the concrete to 1pm. they were sweet with it
rain has eased off a bit. pumps have got the trench mostly dry. still muddy as hell but workable
the stabilised sand delivery showed up early which is good. 2 truck and dogs, about 40 tonne total. dumped it on the stockpile area
the roller is here too, bomag padfoot for compaction of the sub base. operator Dazza is ready when we need him
laying the sub base material in the first section now. about 100mm depth, well compact it to 98% standard
oh theres been a services issue. telstra pit at chainage 480 that wasnt on the plans. we've hit a conduit. stopping work in that area till we get a location done
called dial before you dig, theyve got a locator coming out this afternoon. pain in the arse
the rest of the kerb prep is going ahead though, just working around the telstra issue
compaction testing on the first section. passed. 99% standard proctor. beauty
lunch. weathers cleared up properly now, even some sun. ground is drying out
locator arrived, confirm there's a telstra conduit running diagonally across our alignment at chainage 478 to 485. its only 300mm deep too. way too shallow
gonna have to get a telstra rep out to discuss relocation or protection. this could delay us a couple days in that section. not ideal
emailed the project manager and the client about the telstra issue. sent photos and the locator report
managed to get the formwork done on the first 30m section despite the dramas. ready for concrete tomorrow if the weather holds
kerb forms look good. Kev checked the string line, everything is within tolerance. grade is spot on
end of day. packed up the traffic control for the night shift to set up their layout. theyre doing line marking on the northbound lanes tonight
total crew today was 8 plus traffic controllers. excavator for 3 hours, roller for 2 hours, 2 x pumps all day. got through it considering the weather and the telstra surprise
